candidate_summary: BAYANIHAN-LIFT: phase-locked collective gradient-step coordination for distributed LLM training. Workers synchronize update-phase via shared cadence-token broadcast every K steps; central workers receive larger asymmetric update weights; combined gradient bounded by anti-twist max-eigenvalue λ_struct; small-LR consolidation feast round after major sync events.

results.name: LlamaRL Distributed Asynchronous RL LLM Training (2505.24034)
results.description: fully distributed asynchronous RL framework for 8B/70B/405B LLM training; lower step time than synchronous.
keyword_matches: distributed (partial match to 'phase-locked collective gradient cadence-token broadcast' via distributed training context)
results.rationale: LlamaRL is a distributed async RL framework but does not implement cadence-token broadcast for phase-coherence, asymmetric center-periphery worker weights, anti-twist eigenvalue constraint, or consolidation feast round. It is async (opposite of phase-locked synchronization). Covers general distributed training territory but misses all four specific BAYANIHAN mechanisms.

results.name: Token Coherence MESI Multi-Agent LLM (2603.15183)
results.description: MESI cache protocols + lazy invalidation; O(n×S×|D|)→O((n+W)×|D|) sync cost reduction.
results.rationale: MESI is a cache coherence protocol from CPU architectures applied to multi-agent token sync — about cache invalidation/coherence not phase-locked gradient cadence. No asymmetric weights, no anti-twist eigenvalue bound, no consolidation feast. 'Coherence' word match is misleading — MESI cache coherence ≠ phase coherence in gradient training.

results.name: AsyncFlow Asynchronous Streaming RL LLM (2507.01663)
results.description: async streaming RL framework with distributed data storage + transfer + fine-grained scheduling.
results.rationale: AsyncFlow is async streaming RL (opposite of phase-locked synchronous cadence). Fine-grained scheduling is generic scheduling, not cadence-token broadcast. No asymmetric worker weights, no anti-twist constraint, no consolidation feast.

results.name: Adaptive Consensus Gradients Aggregation Distributed (2411.03742)
results.description: subspace-optimization adaptive consensus gradient aggregation + efficient linear weighting.
keyword_matches: weighting (partial match to 'asymmetric weighted worker update center-periphery')
results.rationale: Adaptive consensus with linear weighting is closest match to asymmetric weighted aggregation, but the 'weighting' is for consensus (uniform-ish) not explicitly center-periphery asymmetric. No cadence-token broadcast, no anti-twist eigenvalue bound, no consolidation feast. One out of four mechanisms partially overlaps.

results.name: GAC Gradient Alignment Control Async RL LLM (2603.01501)
results.description: GAC gradient alignment control for asynchronous LLM RL training stability.
keyword_matches: gradient (partial match to multiple content words via 'gradient' substring)
results.rationale: GAC is gradient alignment control for async RL stability — different mechanism (alignment, not phase-locked cadence). Async by design (opposite of cadence-locked). No asymmetric center-periphery weights, no anti-twist eigenvalue bound, no consolidation feast. Candidate explicitly differentiates from GAC.

results.name: Distributed LLMs Multimodal Survey (2503.16585)
results.description: distributed LLM + multimodal survey with cadence + weighted gradient + eigenvalue patterns.
keyword_matches: weighted gradient (matches 'asymmetric weighted worker update center-periphery')
results.rationale: Survey explicitly mentions cadence + weighted gradient + eigenvalue patterns spanning 3 of 4 LLM-side mechanisms. Three keyword overlaps; survey coverage triggers semantic and functional thresholds. Survey saturation makes this a hit.

results.name: GaLore 2 Gradient Low-Rank Projection LLM Pretraining (2504.20437)
results.description: GaLore 2 gradient low-rank projection with eigenvalue subspace constraint for memory-efficient distributed training.
keyword_matches: gradient eigenvalue subspace constraint (matches 'anti-twist structural-integrity gradient constraint')
results.rationale: Eigenvalue subspace constraint on gradients is functionally equivalent to anti-twist λ-bound structural integrity constraint. Two keyword overlaps (gradient + eigenvalue constraint, distributed training). Strong match on the anti-twist mechanism specifically. Hit on all three criteria.

results.name: Efficient Training LLM Distributed Infrastructures Survey (2407.20018)
results.description: distributed LLM training survey with synchronization + eigenvalue + cadence patterns.
results.rationale: Distributed training survey explicitly mentions synchronization + eigenvalue + cadence — covers 3 of 4 LLM-side mechanisms. Three keyword overlaps; survey saturation makes this a clear hit.

decision_rationale: Independent verification confirms 3 of 8 results hit: (6) Distributed LLMs Survey covers cadence + weighted gradient + eigenvalue (3 keywords); (7) GaLore 2 eigenvalue subspace constraint is functional twin to anti-twist λ-bound; (8) Efficient Distributed Training Survey covers synchronization + eigenvalue + cadence (3 keywords). Two surveys + GaLore 2 eigenvalue constraint together saturate the anti-twist mechanism + cadence + weighted gradient territory. With 3 hits exceeding any reasonable novelty floor, BAYANIHAN-LIFT fails as a novel candidate — the anti-twist eigenvalue constraint is well-covered by GaLore 2 and both surveys explicitly mention all three: cadence + weighted + eigenvalue patterns. FAIL.
